page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of x86_64 system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.cm4.xlarge delivers modest performance, with many metrics falling into the lower percentiles. Stress-ng and Geekbench single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth performance is also limited, with large-block read-only operations registering in the bottom 10% at 21.26 GB/sec, and large-block write operations in the bottom 25% at 14.72 GB/sec. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression at 25.68 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 9.31 GB/sec.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but older Intel Xeon architecture, the ecs.cm4.xlarge offers a stable, predictable execution environment without the risk of CPU credit exhaustion. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for steady-state, low-intensity workloads that require dedicated compute resources but do not demand high-throughput memory or modern instruction sets. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, text processing, lightweight database operations, and legacy application hosting.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor, it provides 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate modest capabilities, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests generally placing in the bottom 25%, though single-threaded compression tasks achieve average tier results. Lacking local storage and high-speed I/O, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for steady-state, low-intensity workloads. It is recommended for general-purpose applications, basic web serving, text processing, and lightweight database operations requiring dedicated but legacy compute resources.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual instance offering balanced compute and memory resources for predictable, low-intensity steady-state workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor with 4 physical cores
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Predictable performance via dedicated CPU allocation under KVM hypervisor
Appropriate for basic web serving, text processing, and legacy applications
